Definition.text: Elusive is a buff state that applies a chance to avoid damage from hits and movement-speed increase at 100% effect.

Purpose.text: Increase movement safety through temporary on-hit risk reduction and mobility.

Core Rules.text: In 3.10, Elusive granted 30% increased movement speed, 15% chance to Dodge attacks, and 15% chance to Dodge spells, replacing earlier values; in 3.16 it now grants 15% chance to avoid Damage from Hits instead of Dodge-related stats.

Internal Rules.text: Elusive applies as a buff effect with current effectiveness; reapplication behavior is patch/skill dependent and can be constrained by source mechanics.

Calculation Order.text: 1) Source grants Elusive; 2) apply immediate bonuses from current effect level; 3) effect is reduced over time; 4) state is removed when effect reaches 0%.

Priority Rules.text: Version-specific behavior replaces previous patch behavior when explicit patch-level change exists (3.10 and 3.16 entries).

Preconditions.text: A source with explicit Elusive-granting effect must apply (for example Mistwalker, Withering Step, skills, or other listed mechanics).

Postconditions.text: When Elusive effect is removed, the entity no longer receives the Elusive bonuses from that instance.

Limitations.text: Historical values differ by patch; cannot merge 3.10 dodge-style wording with 3.16 avoid-from-hits semantics.

Caps.text: Avoid chance from Elusive is capped by the target's global hit-avoid/evade-cap systems (example cited in 3.10: 75% for Avoid Damage from Hits sources).

Formula.text: Observed model (from PoE Wiki): effective movement-speed and hit-avoid bonuses are proportional to current Elusive effectiveness; effectiveness decays over time after application.

prevents: using outdated dodge-based Elusive values after 3.16

Conflicts.text: Community and in-game observation have historically diverged from textual wording around decay and reapplication windows; patch-level changes should be prioritized.

Exceptions.text: Withering Step and other mechanics can replace or reapply Elusive behavior by their explicit rule sets.

Hidden Rules.text: Avoid from Elusive is not identical wording to legacy dodge systems; treat it as the post-3.16 avoid-from-hit effect in patch-specific records.

Developer Notes.text: Model Elusive across two historical branches (dodge branch pre-3.16, avoid-from-hit branch post-3.16) and avoid blending in same expression unless continuity is explicitly declared.

Historical Changes.text: 3.10 adjusted Elusive stats (30% MS; 15%/15% dodge) and 3.16 converted Elusive to 15% chance to Avoid Damage from Hits with old dodge wording removed.

text.note: Elusive now grants 30% increased movement speed, 15% chance to dodge attacks, and 15% chance to dodge spells.
Elusive changed to grant 15% chance to Avoid Damage from Hits instead of dodge-related stats.

text: At 100% Elusive effect in 3.16+, incoming hits are avoided with 15% chance from the Elusive effect.
Pre-3.16 Elusive examples use dodge-style wording and are not equivalent to post-3.16 behavior.
Using post-3.16 Avoid-from-Hits semantics to represent pre-3.16 dodge wording.
Assuming a fixed-duration buff model instead of observed effectiveness decay behavior in historical sources.

modifies: chance to avoid damage from hits
